issues: The screenshot search query field ('likelySearchQuery') reads like an internal note and should not be published as-is.
The title is a bit long/list-like and could be tightened for readability.
The rootCause is set to low, which is fine; no blocking issue here.
Consider softening any wording that implies the fake .png is definitely web-accessible, since that is only a possibility in the evidence.
fixes: Remove or hide the 'likelySearchQuery' text from any public-facing page.
Shorten the title to something like: 'WordPress functions.php Credential Stealer Saving Logins as Fake PNG'.
Keep the root-cause language explicit that the original intrusion vector is unknown.
Where you mention the fake .png accessibility, say 'may be accessible' instead of stating or implying it was publicly reachable.
